agent_raw:
The graph detector migration packet does not justify replacement of the existing system. The comparison has four embedded defects.

Feed mismatch: The baseline numbers are anchored to the nightly batch settlement feed (what ops dashboards use). The challenger is scored on the authorization stream with deduplication after merchant entity resolution. Comparing metrics across different transaction feeds is not a model quality comparison.

Label maturity mismatch: The baseline includes transactions with unsettled disputes in the label pool. The challenger evaluation restricts to transactions with 60-day settled dispute outcomes, which excludes recent fraudulent transactions still in the pipeline. This changes the effective positive-label rate and difficulty of the evaluation.

Threshold self-selection: The Q4 operating threshold was calibrated to maximize precision at the target investigation volume on Q4 data. Reporting precision at this threshold on Q4 data is circular — the threshold was chosen to maximize the reported metric.

Model-and-pipeline bundled: The challenger changes the model class (graph model) and the transaction-processing pipeline (authorization stream, deduplication, entity resolution) simultaneously. The attribution of precision gains to the model versus the pipeline is impossible from the current packet.

A fair comparison requires rescoring both systems on one feed, one label-maturity rule, and thresholds fixed from pre-Q4 data.
all_issues_raised: Transaction feed changed from settlement batch to deduplicated authorization stream
Label maturation changed from full dispute stream to 60-day settled-only
Q4 threshold calibrated on Q4 and reported on Q4 — circular
Model class and pipeline changed together — gains not attributable to model alone
